rows.source_ref: Eliteforexpartner, 'A Scalping/Day Trading strategy 5minute timeframe', forums.babypips.com, thread #1266726, 2024, https://forums.babypips.com/t/a-scalping-day-trading-strategy-5minute-timeframe/1266726
rows.concept: A MACD zero-line cross sets trade direction ('campaign'); after price pulls back into a Bollinger Band, a buy/sell stop 1 pip beyond the pre-pullback swing extreme catches the momentum-continuation breakout.
rows.eligibility_reason: Mechanical M5 breakout-after-pullback: MACD(6,17,1) zero-cross direction filter, Bollinger(10,shift 1,dev 0.66) pullback, stop order 1 pip past marked swing high/low, defined SL/TP variants (1:1 or 2:1) - pure MQL5, deterministic, defined stops, DXZ/FTMO-compatible.
rows.notes: Low edge plausibility: promotional source (poster's blog link in bio), forum member TheodoreThring shows MACD-6/17/1 reduces exactly to an EMA-6/17 crossover, and tester DEAMON reports 'terrible results' / 'cannot be traded like this, needs filters'. Generic trend-continuation breakout, no distinctive uncorrelated driver. Three exit methods: M1 SL 1 pip below breakout candle at 1:1 RR, M2 SL 1 pip below lower band at 2:1 RR (both mechanical), M3 discretionary trend exit (wedge break / third-touch overshoot) - excluded as non-deterministic. 'Draw resistance at pre-pullback high' = swing/pivot-high detection, mechanizable. Wick-above-high without close = re-mark high (repeat step 3). Cancel unfilled stop order on opposite zero-line cross. Author claims it also works on indices and precious metals.
